title: Electroporation of Thalassiosira pseudonana
author: Joshua Bugge
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.yvqfw5w

steps.title: Grow cultures of T
steps.instruction: Grow cultures of T. psuedonana on f/2 supplemented sterile seawater to a density of approximately 1.1 x 10 6 cells mL -1

steps.title: All procedures are carried out at...
steps.instruction: All procedures are carried out at 4 C and cells were kept on ice. Collect cells from 500 mL of culture (see step 1) by centrifugation for 10 m in at 3000 x g in 10 x 50 mL conical centrifuge tubes. Discard supernatant and resuspend pellet in 1 mL of 375 mM sorbitol (filter sterilized)

steps.title: Combine resuspended cells into one 50...
steps.instruction: Combine resuspended cells into one 50 mL conical centrifuge tube and centrifuge at 3000 x g for 10 min. Discard supernatant and resuspend pellet in 800 uL of 375 mM sorbitol (filter sterilized). Transfer to 1.5 mL tube and store on ice.

steps.title: All procedures are carried out at...
steps.instruction: All procedures are carried out at 4 C and cells were kept on ice. Concentrate cells by vacuum filtration to approximately 300 mL. Collect cells by centrifugation in 50 mL conical centrifugation tubes at 3000 x g for 10 min. Discard supernatnat and resuspend pellet in in 2 mL of 375 mM sorbitol (filter sterilized).

steps.title: Combine cells into new tube and...
steps.instruction: Combine cells into new tube and centrifuge at 3000 x g for 10 min. Discard supernatant and resuspend in 1 mM of 375 mM sorbitol.

steps.title: On ice, add linearized plasmid (in...
steps.instruction: On ice, add linearized plasmid (in 250 mM sorbitol) to a final concentration of 0.15 µg mL -1 tothe concentrated cells from above.

steps.title: Transfer 100 µL of cells +...
steps.instruction: Transfer 100 µL of cells + plasmid to a pre-chilled 2 mm gap electroporation cuvette.

steps.title: Wipe cuvette dry with kimwipe and...
steps.instruction: Wipe cuvette dry with kimwipe and place into Biorad Gene Pulser electroporator set to 25 µF and 400 ohm resistance. In our hands, the highest transformation efficiencies were obtained using a field strength o 2.50 kv/cm.

steps.title: Transfer electroporated cells to 10 mL...
steps.instruction: Transfer electroporated cells to 10 mL of sterile, f/2 supplemented artiicial seawater and incubate overnight at 17 C under constant light (170 µE m -2 s -1 ).

steps.title: The following day, plate ca
steps.instruction: The following day, plate ca. 5 x 10 8 cells on selective NEPC agar plates (in our experiment we used 100 µg mL -1 NAT). Controls should include no-plasmid and no electroporation treatments.

steps.title: Grow plates under constant light (170...
steps.instruction: Grow plates under constant light (170 µE m -2 s -1 ). Colonies appear approximately 10 days after plating.

steps.title: Transfer colonies to 300 µL of...
steps.instruction: Transfer colonies to 300 µL of f/2 supplemented artificial seawater in 96 well plates. Optical denisty (600 or 680 nm) can be used as a proxy for growth using a plate reader.
